description: Comprehension questions for Calor vs C# evaluation — 100+ questions across all 40 programs

questions.question: What side effect does this program have?
questions.answer: It writes to the console (prints 'Hello from Calor!')

questions.question: What does the effect annotation §E{cw} indicate?
questions.answer: Console write — the function performs console output

questions.question: What is the return type of the Main function?
questions.answer: void — it returns nothing

questions.question: How many functions does this module contain?
questions.answer: 4 functions: Add, Subtract, Multiply, Divide

questions.question: Does the Divide function have any safety contracts?
questions.answer: No, it has no preconditions — division by zero is unguarded

questions.question: What side effects do any of these functions have?
questions.answer: None — all functions are pure (no §E{} declarations)

questions.question: What does this function return for input 15?
questions.answer: FizzBuzz

questions.question: What are the loop bounds?
questions.answer: 1 to n inclusive

questions.question: In what order are divisibility checks performed?
questions.answer: First checks if divisible by both 3 and 5 (15), then by 3 alone, then by 5 alone

questions.question: What happens for input 7?
questions.answer: Returns the string representation of 7

questions.question: What algorithm does this use — recursion or iteration?
questions.answer: Iteration — it uses a while loop (§WH) with a counter

questions.question: What is the initial value of the result variable?
Does this function have any preconditions?
questions.answer: No — there are no §Q contracts, so negative inputs are unguarded

questions.question: What does the function return for input 0 or 1?
questions.answer: Returns n itself (0 or 1)

questions.question: What control flow structures are used?
questions.answer: An if-else (§IF/§EL) for the base case and a while loop (§WH) for iteration

questions.question: How does the iterative approach track state?
questions.answer: Using three mutable variables: a (previous), b (current), and i (counter), swapping at each step

questions.question: What precondition must be satisfied to call Divide?
questions.answer: The divisor (b) must not be zero

questions.question: What does the ensures clause guarantee?
questions.answer: The result equals the quotient of a divided by b

questions.question: What effect annotation does this function have?
questions.answer: Pure — it has no side effects (no §E{} declaration)

questions.question: How many string utility functions are provided?
questions.answer: 4: GetLength, ToUpper, ToLower, Concat

questions.question: What does the GetLength function use to determine string length?
questions.answer: The (len s) built-in function

questions.question: Do any of these functions have side effects?
questions.answer: No — all are pure functions with no effect declarations

questions.question: What postcondition does the Abs function guarantee?
questions.answer: The result is >= 0

questions.question: How does Abs handle negative numbers without a built-in abs?
questions.answer: Uses an if expression: if x < 0 return (0 - x), else return x

questions.question: How many functions have postconditions (§S)?
questions.answer: One — only Abs has a postcondition (result >= 0)

questions.question: How does the Sum function add three numbers?
questions.answer: Nested addition: (+ (+ a b) c)

questions.question: What is the difference between Sum and SumTwo?
questions.answer: Sum takes 3 parameters, SumTwo takes 2

questions.question: How does Max3 determine the maximum of three numbers?
questions.answer: Nested if-else: compares a vs b first, then the winner against c

questions.question: Where does the Max function end?
questions.answer: At §/F{f001} — the closing tag explicitly marks the boundary

questions.question: What does the function return for input 1?
questions.answer: false

questions.question: Why does the loop increment by 2?
questions.answer: Because even numbers (except 2) cannot be prime, so only odd divisors need to be checked

questions.question: What is the precondition for calling IsPrime?
questions.answer: The input n must be greater than 0

questions.question: What does Find return when the target matches val2?
questions.answer: 1 (the index of the second value)

questions.question: What does Find return when the target is not found?
How many comparisons does this function make in the worst case?
questions.answer: 3 — it checks val1, then val2, then val3 sequentially using §EI

questions.question: What is the formula used for CelsiusToFahrenheit?
How many conversion functions are provided?
questions.answer: 3: CelsiusToFahrenheit, FahrenheitToCelsius, CelsiusToKelvin

questions.question: Do any functions have preconditions on temperature values?
questions.answer: No — there are no §Q contracts, so any f64 value is accepted

questions.question: What does IsLeapYear return for year 2000?
questions.answer: true — 2000 is divisible by 400

questions.question: In what order are the divisibility checks performed?
questions.answer: First 400, then 100, then 4 — using §IF/§EI/§EI/§EL chain

questions.question: What happens for year 1900?
questions.answer: Returns false — 1900 is divisible by 100 but not 400

questions.question: What algorithm is used?
questions.answer: Euclidean algorithm using modulo — iterates while y != 0

questions.question: What is the loop termination condition?
questions.answer: The loop continues while y is not equal to 0 (!= y 0)

questions.question: How are the values swapped each iteration?
questions.answer: temp = y, y = x % y, x = temp

questions.question: What does the Swap function do?
questions.answer: Concatenates b and a in reversed order

questions.question: What does the Identity function do?
questions.answer: Returns the input string unchanged

questions.question: Which characters are considered vowels?
questions.answer: a, e, i, o, u (lowercase only)

questions.question: Does IsVowel handle uppercase vowels?
questions.answer: No — it only checks lowercase letters

questions.question: What control flow pattern is used for the multiple comparisons?
questions.answer: An if/else-if chain using §IF/§EI/§EI/§EI/§EI/§EL

questions.question: What does IsPalindrome3 check?
questions.answer: Whether the first and third characters (a and c) are equal

questions.question: How many functions are in this module?
questions.answer: 2: IsPalindrome3 and AreEqual

questions.question: What precondition does the Power function require?
questions.answer: The exponent (exp) must be >= 0

questions.question: What inputs will cause this function to throw a contract violation?
questions.answer: Any negative exponent value (exp < 0)

questions.question: What is the algorithm used?
questions.answer: Iterative multiplication — multiplies result by base, exp times

questions.question: What does Clamp(5, 1, 10) return?
questions.answer: 5 (unchanged, since it's within the range)

questions.question: What are the two postconditions (ensures clauses)?
questions.answer: The result is >= min and <= max

questions.question: What is the precondition for the min and max parameters?
questions.answer: min must be less than or equal to max

questions.question: How does NewCount determine the updated count?
questions.answer: If isPush is true, adds 1 to oldCount; otherwise subtracts 1

questions.question: What does IsEmpty return?
questions.answer: true if count equals 0, false otherwise

questions.question: Does this module have contracts to prevent popping from an empty stack?
questions.answer: No — there are no preconditions (§Q) on NewCount to guard against negative counts

questions.question: What is the structural difference between Queue and Stack modules?
questions.answer: They are structurally identical — both use isPush/isEnqueue boolean to determine increment/decrement

questions.question: Does this queue have any side effects?
questions.answer: No — all functions are pure with no effect declarations

questions.question: What functions does the LinkedList module provide?
questions.answer: 3 functions: IsEmpty, Size, and NewSize

questions.question: How does NewSize calculate the updated size?
questions.answer: Adds 1 if isAdd is true, subtracts 1 otherwise

questions.question: What does CompareValues return for equal inputs?
How does the module determine tree traversal direction?
questions.answer: ShouldGoLeft returns true if value < nodeValue, ShouldGoRight if value > nodeValue

questions.question: What does CompareValues return when a < b?
What does SetValue return?
questions.answer: The newValue parameter — it replaces the old value

questions.question: Does this implementation have any thread-safety contracts?
questions.answer: No — there are no contracts or synchronization mechanisms

questions.question: What preconditions does RectangleArea require?
questions.answer: Both width > 0 and height > 0

questions.question: What postcondition is shared across all area functions?
questions.answer: result >= 0 — all areas must be non-negative

questions.question: What inputs will cause CircleArea to throw a contract violation?
questions.answer: Any radius <= 0 (the precondition requires radius > 0.0)

questions.question: How many shape calculation functions are defined?
questions.answer: 5: CircleArea, RectangleArea, SquareArea, CirclePerimeter, RectanglePerimeter

questions.question: What preconditions does ItemTotal enforce?
questions.answer: price > 0 and quantity > 0

questions.question: What constraints does the TotalWithTax function place on taxRate?
questions.answer: taxRate must be >= 0 and <= 1.0 (0% to 100%)

questions.question: What guarantee does TotalWithTax make about its result?
questions.answer: The result is >= subtotal (tax can only increase the total)

questions.question: What happens in NewQuantity when the result would be negative?
questions.answer: It returns 0 instead — quantity cannot go below zero

questions.question: How many preconditions (§Q) are in the entire module?
questions.answer: 7 preconditions across 4 functions

questions.question: What does ChoosePivot return?
questions.answer: The high index — it uses the last element as pivot

questions.question: What preconditions does ChoosePivot require?
questions.answer: low >= 0 and low <= high

questions.question: What does ShouldSwap determine?
questions.answer: Whether a value should be placed before the pivot (value <= pivot)

questions.question: What postconditions does the Max function guarantee?
questions.answer: result >= a AND result >= b

questions.question: What are all contracts on the Clamp function?
questions.answer: Precondition: min <= max. Postconditions: result >= min AND result <= max

questions.question: Can you verify that Abs always satisfies result >= 0?
questions.answer: Yes — when x >= 0, result = x >= 0; when x < 0, result = -x > 0

questions.question: Why does BadAbs violate its postcondition?
questions.answer: It returns x directly without negating negative values, so result can be < 0

questions.question: For what inputs does BadMax violate result >= b?
questions.answer: When b > a — BadMax always returns a regardless of comparison

questions.question: Why does BadDivide always violate its postcondition?
questions.answer: It always returns 0 instead of a/b — the ensures clause (result == a/b) fails for most inputs

questions.question: Which functions have provably correct contracts?
questions.answer: GoodSquare (x*x >= 0), SafeDivide (with != 0 guard), and ComplexCondition (x+y > 0 when both > 0)

questions.question: Why is BadNegate's postcondition (result < 0) not always true?
questions.answer: When x = 0, (- 0 x) = 0 which is not < 0

questions.question: What message is shown if SafeDivide's precondition is violated?
questions.answer: divisor must not be zero

questions.question: Why can IncrementUnsafe violate result > x?
questions.answer: Integer overflow: when x = 2147483647 (max i32), x+1 wraps to -2147483648

questions.question: What input causes SquareUnsafe to violate result >= 0?
questions.answer: Large values like x = 46341 cause x*x to overflow, producing a negative result

questions.question: How many functions in this module have unsafe contracts?
questions.answer: 6 — all functions have contracts that can be violated due to integer overflow

questions.question: How does IncrementSafe prevent overflow?
questions.answer: Precondition: x < 2147483647 — prevents x+1 from wrapping

questions.question: What bound does SquareSafe use to prevent overflow?
questions.answer: x <= 46340 — because 46340^2 = 2147395600 fits in i32, but 46341^2 overflows

questions.question: How many preconditions does AddPositivesSafe have?
What does ConcatLonger's postcondition guarantee?
questions.answer: The result length is greater than the length of b (since non-empty a is prepended)

questions.question: What does ContainsImpliesLength verify?
questions.answer: If haystack contains needle, then haystack length >= needle length

questions.question: What precondition does NonEmptyLength require?
questions.answer: The string must not be empty: (! (isempty s))

questions.question: What does ValidIndex check?
questions.answer: Whether index is in range [0, length) — both >= 0 and < length

questions.question: What does MidpointIndex guarantee about its result?
questions.answer: result >= low AND result < high — the midpoint is always within bounds

questions.question: How does MidpointIndex avoid integer overflow?
questions.answer: Uses low + (high - low) / 2 instead of (low + high) / 2

questions.question: Which functions have console write effects?
questions.answer: LogMessage and LogAndCompute — both declare §E{cw}

questions.question: Is ComputeSum a pure function?
questions.answer: Yes — it has no effect declaration and only performs arithmetic

questions.question: Does LogAndCompute correctly declare all its effects?
questions.answer: Yes — it prints to console and declares §E{cw}

questions.question: Does HiddenNetwork have any undeclared effects?
questions.answer: The function is named HiddenNetwork but only returns the url — it has no actual side effects

questions.question: What effect does UndeclaredWrite declare?
questions.answer: §E{cw} — console write, though it seems to represent a file write conceptually

questions.question: What functions does ProcessOrder call?
questions.answer: SaveOrder and NotifyCustomer — through §C{} call syntax

questions.question: Does ProcessOrder correctly declare its effects?
questions.answer: It declares §E{cw} — matching its transitive calls which all use console write

questions.question: What arguments are passed to NotifyCustomer?
questions.answer: email and the string literal 'Order shipped'

questions.question: Which function has a side effect?
questions.answer: LogMessage — it declares §E{cw} and prints the message

questions.question: Are ConcatStrings, GetLength, and ToUpperCase pure?
questions.answer: Yes — they have no effect declarations and only transform data

questions.question: Do any functions in this module have side effects?
questions.answer: No — all 6 functions are pure with no effect declarations

questions.question: What is the only function with a precondition?
questions.answer: Power — requires exp >= 0

questions.question: How many functions are defined in this module?
questions.answer: 6: Add, Multiply, Abs, Max, Power, StringConcat
